articles.title: Iran's military says it destroyed US drone
articles.summary: Iran's military says it destroyed US drone The Iranian army said its naval air defence systems had shot down a US-made Lucas drone. Iran’s IRNA news agency published a photograph of what it said was the damaged drone. Middle East Eye could not independently verify the claim. The Lucas is the US version of the low-cost Shahed one-way attack drone.

articles.title: Iran executes two men over killing of police officers
articles.summary: Iran executes two men over killing of police officers Iran has executed two men convicted of killing four police officers during anti-government unrest in the central city of Isfahan in January, according to the judiciary-affiliated Mizan news agency. Mizan identified the men as Erfan Esfandiyari and Golmohammad Mohammadi, saying their death sentences were carried out after court proceedings. They were convicted of attacking the officers, tying them to a road sign, assaulting them with stones, dousing them with petrol and setting them on fire. articles.title: Jordan said no evacuation of Aqaba airport or seaport after US warning
articles.summary: Jordan said no evacuation of Aqaba airport or seaport after US warning Jordan denied reports that Aqaba airport and seaport had been evacuated after the US embassy warned Americans of a “credible threat”. Jordanian government spokesman Mohammad al-Momani told the Petra news agency that “relevant authorities have not issued any evacuation orders at the Aqaba airport or seaport”. Momani said authorities had not recorded any potential threats to Aqaba over the previous several hours. Both the airport and seaport were operating normally, he added.

articles.title: Trump says he ‘couldn’t care less’ about Iran leaving nuclear agreement
articles.summary: Trump says he ‘couldn’t care less’ about Iran leaving nuclear agreement US President Donald Trump said he “couldn’t care less” after Iran announced it would no longer abide by the US-Iran memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed last month. Speaking in a brief phone interview with US cable network NewsNation, Trump reiterated that the US’s main objective remains “never allowing Iran to have a nuclear weapon”. Washington and Tehran signed the MoU in mid-June in an effort to end the war launched by the US and Israel in February.

Lebanese president hopes to generate pressure on Israel during US trip
articles.summary: Lebanese president hopes to generate pressure on Israel during US trip  Lebanese President Joseph Aoun will make his first trip to the White House this week to present a plan to US president Donald Trump on how to disarm Hezbollah and secure Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon. Aoun is the first Lebanese head of state in nearly 20 years to visit the White House, where he will meet Trump face-to-face for the first time. Tuesday's meeting comes at a crucial moment for Lebanon: Israeli troops are occupying a swathe of the country's south, hundreds of thousands of Lebanese remain displaced follo

articles.title: Israeli military closes area around Gaza ahead of settler march, report says
articles.summary: Israeli military closes area around Gaza ahead of settler march, report says Israeli military has declared the entire Gaza periphery region a closed military zone, ahead of a march planned by a right-wing group that seeks to reestablish Israeli settlements inside the territory, Israeli media reported. The March of Thousands, organised by the Nahala settler group, was expected to start from the Yad Mordechai area at 5 pm local time, the Times of Israel reported. The organisers had the goal of “returning home after 21 years,” a reference to the removal of Israeli settlements inside Gaza.

articles.title: Emergency services on alert in Iran's earthquake-hit Khuzestan
articles.summary: Emergency services on alert in Iran's earthquake-hit Khuzestan Iranian authorities have put emergency services on alert in Khuzestan province, which was hit by a magnitude 5 earthquake this morning. Khuzestan governor, Seyed Mohammad Reza Mowlaizadeh, said he has issued the “necessary instructions for the full mobilisation of rescue and operational forces”, Al Jazeera reported. Mowlaizadeh said no casualties or significant financial damage had yet been reported in the region, and official teams were assessing the situation.
